text:
Happy to be here, and greet you with the warmest of Christian love, in the Name of our Lord Jesus Christ, Who suffered for us, the Innocent for we guilty. And one time being alienated from God, now has brought us nigh unto Him by the shedding of His blood, that we're called sons and daughters of God. It does not yet appear what we shall be in the final end, but we'll know we'll have a body like His, for we shall see Him as He is. That's worth it all, isn't it? That's right.
Well, last evening I was thrilled to my heart was almost standing still, when I looked and seen that tent full of people seeking God. That was a greater thrill to me than anything I've seen in a long time. It's the first time I made an altar call, I guess, since I left Africa.
One--the altar call there consist of thirty thousand got saved at one time; and then--when they had seen the glory of our Lord. And that was about the second altar call of that type that I've had since I--I left Africa.
text_no:
Det er en glede å være her, og jeg hilser dere med den varmeste kristne kjærlighet, i vår Herre Jesu Kristi navn, Han som led for oss, den Uskyldige for de skyldige. En gang var vi fremmedgjort fra Gud, men nå har Han brakt oss nær ved å utøse Sitt blod, slik at vi kalles Guds sønner og døtre. Det er ennå ikke åpenbart hva vi skal bli til slutt, men vi vet at vi skal ha en kropp som Hans, for vi skal se Ham slik Han er. Det gjør det hele verdt det, ikke sant? Ja, det er riktig.
I går kveld ble jeg så begeistret at hjertet nesten stod stille, da jeg så teltet fullt av mennesker som søkte Gud. Det var en større glede for meg enn noe annet jeg har sett på lenge. Dette var første gang jeg kalte inn til alteret, tror jeg, siden jeg forlot Afrika.
En gang—kalte til alteret der, hvor tretti tusen ble frelst på én gang, da de så vår Herres herlighet. Og dette var omtrent den andre alterkall av denne typen jeg har hatt siden jeg forlot Afrika.

text:
I remember we was standing in Durban, and there was about some hundred thousand, close to it, gathered in. We couldn't see what was down the street for three or four city blocks. And so there were many doctors and people setting around. And upon a platform built out… It was a racetrack in this, but it was much larger than Churchill Downs. Durban has the second largest in the world. London, England, has the first--the largest racetrack, then Durban, South Africa. I think Churchill Downs ranks fourth, or third, or fourth, fifth, somewhere along then.
So the place was just jammed with people all out across the track this way and all, and the stand was built way out in the field. Then for three city blocks, down every--every aisle they were setting in the streets, for three city blocks. They couldn't get the people in. And many of them packing little mud idols and claimed to be Christians, going along, but when…
text_no:
Jeg husker vi sto i Durban, hvor omtrent hundre tusen mennesker hadde samlet seg. Vi kunne ikke se nedover gaten på grunn av folkemengden som strakte seg tre eller fire kvartaler. Mange leger og andre satt rundt omkring. På en plattform bygget ut over et travbaneområde, mye større enn Churchill Downs. Durban har den nest største travbanen i verden. London, England, har den største, og deretter kommer Durban, Sør-Afrika. Jeg tror Churchill Downs ligger på tredje, fjerde eller femte plass. 
Stedet var stappfullt av mennesker, både ute på banen og på tribunen som var bygget langt ut på feltet. For tre kvartaler nedover var gatene fylt med folk som satt overalt, siden det ikke var plass til alle. Mange bar små leirfigurer og hevdet å være kristne.

text:
That afternoon the mayor of the city brought me out, and I--talking to a man, a colored man there, he was just… I said, "Why are they packing them--them tags?"
Said, "That means they're Christians."
I said, "Well there's a man packing an idol, and still he's a Christian?"
Said, "Oh, yes." Said, "Would you like to talk him?" He said, "He's a Zulu; I can talk to him."
I said, "All right."
Now, he--we pulled the car in. He said, "Just call him John or anything you want to call him. He'll--he'll answer you."
So, and I called the--a name; well then, he answered through the interpreter of course. And I said, "Are you a Christian?"
"Oh, yeah." He was a Christian. And I said, "Well, why the idol?" had blood sprinkled all over it, little mud figures. Oh, they had all kind of freakish looking things.
And I said, "Well, what's the--the… What about the idols?"
"Well, that's god too." See?
And I said, "God too? There's only one God." See?
And he said, "Oh, that was god." Said his father packed it.
And I said, "Well, what--what'd your father pack it for?"
Said, "Well, one day the lion got after him, and--and he built up a little fire and said a prayer, and the lion went away. So it was a god of power." So he packed it too. If the Christian God failed, then he'd go to that one. Not very much Christianity there. You see?
So then, I said, "There's nothing in that idol."
text_no:
Den ettermiddagen tok byens ordfører meg med ut, og jeg pratet med en mann, en farget mann der. Jeg spurte ham, "Hvorfor bærer de disse merkene?"
Han svarte, "Det betyr at de er kristne."
Jeg sa, "Men der er en mann som bærer på en idol, og han er likevel kristen?"
Han sa, "Å ja, vil du snakke med ham?" Han la til, "Han er en Zulu; jeg kan snakke med ham for deg."
Jeg sa, "Greit."
Vi stoppet bilen, og han sa, "Bare kall ham John eller noe annet, han vil svare deg."
Jeg kalte ham ved et navn, og han svarte gjennom tolken. Jeg spurte, "Er du en kristen?"
"Å ja," svarte han. Han var kristen. Jeg fortsatte, "Men hvorfor har du med deg en idol?" Den hadde blodstrint over seg og små gjørmefigurer. De hadde alle slags merkelige ting.
Jeg spurte, "Hva med idolene?"
"Vel, det er også gud," svarte han.
Jeg protesterte, "Gud også? Det finnes bare én Gud."
Han forklarte, "Det var gud. Min far bar den."
Jeg spurte, "Hvorfor bar din far den?"
Han svarte, "En dag ble han jaget av en løve, han tentet et bål og sa en bønn, og løven gikk vekk. Så det var en gud med makt." Derfor bar han også den. Hvis den kristne Gud sviktet, ville han gå til den. Ikke mye kristendom der, ser du.
Så sa jeg, "Det er ingenting i den idolen."

text:
But that day, the first one on the platform, I'll never forget. They couldn't give out prayer cards; we just had the missionaries to go out and get so many out of each group and bring them to the platform. And there was…
I remember the first woman passed across the--what they told me, and the tapes reading them back. There was a woman who had had a--a female disorder. And she was a Mohammedan; she had a red dot between her eyes. There's some twenty or thirty thousand of those over, way over to my left (See?), in that direction: and very staunch Mohammedans. (Mahatma Gandhi's boy was there.) And so they said. They have a red dot; the priest puts it between their eyes; and that means that they deny the existence that Jesus was the Son of God. They believe in God, Jehovah God, but they believe Mohammed was His prophet (You see?), and not Jesus. Said Jesus a good man, but He--He wasn't the--the real prophet that--that Mohammed was. In Jerusalem they got a wall built, and on one end they got a little, bitty tower, that's--that's where Jesus will come to. And they have a great big tower; that's where Mohammed's a coming. You talk about superstitions, this world certainly has got them.
text_no: Den dagen, den første på plattformen, vil jeg aldri glemme. De kunne ikke dele ut bønnekort; vi lot bare misjonærene gå ut og hente et visst antall fra hver gruppe og bringe dem til plattformen. Jeg husker den første kvinnen som kom over, slik de fortalte meg, og som jeg senere hørte på lydbåndene. Hun hadde en kvinnesykdom og var en mohammedaner. Hun hadde en rød prikk mellom øynene. Det var kanskje tjue eller tretti tusen av dem langt til venstre for meg, i den retningen: og svært strenge mohammedanere. (Mahatma Gandhis sønn var der også.) De sa at den røde prikken settes mellom øynene av en prest, og det betyr at de benekter at Jesus var Guds Sønn. De tror på Gud, Jehova Gud, men de mener Mohammed var Hans profet, ikke Jesus. De sier at Jesus var en god mann, men Han var ikke den virkelige profeten som Mohammed var. I Jerusalem har de bygd en mur, og på den ene enden har de et lite tårn; det er der Jesus skal komme. Og de har et stort tårn; det er der Mohammed skal komme. Når det gjelder overtro, er denne verden virkelig gjennomsyret av det.

text:
Then when--when this woman come up, why--they had talk to an interpreter, and they said, asked… I asked her if she believed. She said, "Yes." So it told her about what was her trouble.
She looked at me; she said, "That's right," (See?) through the interpreter, of course.
And I said, "Your husband was with you when you went to the doctor."
She said, "Yes."
I said, "He's wearing a gray suit, wasn't he? Doesn't he have a mustache?"
Said, "Yes." That was right. See?
And so I said, "Why did you come to me, and--and why didn't you go to the priest at the temple?"
She said she believed. And I said, "Well, you believe in--in Christ?"
And she said, "Karishener."
And I said, "Not Karishener, Christ." Karishener is their incarnated god. I said, "Not Karishener, Christ." And she--she said… I said, "Well now, do you believe me to be the--His servant?"
"Yes."
I said, "Then if I be His servant, I speak of Jesus Christ." I said, "Will you accept Him as your Saviour?"
She said, "Yes," she would. And then we prayed for her.
text_no:
Da denne kvinnen kom opp, måtte de bruke en tolk, og jeg spurte henne om hun trodde. Hun sa "Ja." Jeg fortalte henne hva som var hennes problem.
Hun så på meg og sa, "Det stemmer," gjennom tolken, selvfølgelig.
Jeg sa, "Din ektemann var med deg da du gikk til legen."
Hun sa, "Ja."
Jeg sa, "Han hadde på seg en grå dress, ikke sant? Og har han ikke bart?"
Hun sa, "Ja." Det stemte.
Så spurte jeg, "Hvorfor kom du til meg i stedet for å gå til presten i tempelet?"
Hun sa at hun trodde. Jeg spurte, "Tror du på Kristus?"
Hun sa, "Karishener."
Jeg svarte, "Ikke Karishener, men Kristus." Karishener er deres inkarnert gud. Jeg sa, "Ikke Karishener, Kristus."
Jeg spurte henne, "Tror du at jeg er Hans tjener?"
"Ja."
Jeg sa, "Da jeg er Hans tjener, taler jeg om Jesus Kristus. Vil du akseptere Ham som din Frelser?"
Hun sa, "Ja," hun ville. Deretter ba vi for henne.

text:
Then the next one come was a woman with a very odd thing. The woman didn't have hardly, I forget, just a little something didn't amount to much. But when the woman was standing there, it begin to get real dark around her. And I watched it; it faded out; and I seen them packing her away. Now, many times I see death at the person, but I won't say nothing about it; I just say, "The Lord bless you," pray for them, send them on away. See? 'Cause prayer could change that. See? Hezekiah did in his case, didn't it? It changed it. So I won't tell them unless I see that it's already… This woman was being packed to the grave.
And I said, told her what her trouble was, and she said, "That's right." And she was a--an Afrikaans woman. Which, an African… We usually think; we think of Afrikaans, he's a--he's a black man, but an Afrikaans is a white man. An African is a black man, called Kafir.
But this--this woman, when she came, and was telling her what her trouble was, very minor, but I said, "Lady, you prepare for death, because you're not going to live but just a little bit." In less than twenty minutes she was a corpse. See? Walked right off the platform and set down, begin to say, "Well, now…" She was talking to her husband and them out there and said, "I can't understand it; I've never had no sickness, just a--maybe a little cyst or something." And--and in a few minutes she was standing there laughing about something, and just right over she went, and that was it. She died right there. And see? If--if I'd been a healer, I'd have healed her. See, see? I'm not the healer; God is the Healer. See? So I could only say what He told me.
text_no:
Den neste som kom, var en kvinne med et veldig merkelig problem. Kvinnen hadde knapt noe galt, bare en liten bagatell. Men da kvinnen stod der, begynte det å bli virkelig mørkt rundt henne. Jeg så på det, og så at det bleknet bort. Jeg så dem bære henne bort. Mange ganger ser jeg døden rundt en person, men jeg sier ingenting om det. Jeg sier bare: "Herren velsigne deg," ber for dem og sender dem videre. For bønn kan forandre situasjonen, som i tilfelle med Hiskia. Så jeg sier ingenting med mindre jeg ser at det allerede er bestemt. Denne kvinnen ble båret til graven.
Jeg fortalte henne hva problemet hennes var, og hun sa, "Det stemmer." Hun var en afrikansk kvinne. Vi tenker vanligvis på afrikanere som svarte mennesker, men en afrikander er en hvit person, mens en afrikaner er en svart person, også kjent som en kaffir.
Denne kvinnen hadde et mindre problem, men jeg sa, "Dame, gjør deg klar for døden, for du har ikke lenge igjen å leve." Mindre enn tjue minutter senere var hun død. Hun gikk av plattformen, satte seg ned og begynte å snakke med mannen sin og de andre der ute, og sa: "Jeg forstår det ikke; jeg har aldri vært syk, bare kanskje hatt en liten cyste eller noe." 
Få minutter senere stod hun der og lo av noe, og så falt hun om, og det var det. Hun døde rett der. Hvis jeg hadde vært en helbreder, ville jeg ha helbredet henne. Men jeg er ikke helbrederen; Gud er Helbrederen. Jeg kunne bare si det Han fortalte meg.

text:
And then, the next coming through was a little black boy. Now, some of you little children here, when you go out here to this fountain, and drink, and the many things you have, you know how little African boys have to drink? They lay right down and drink anywhere they can find some water; no matter if it's muddy or what it is, they have to drink it anyhow; they haven't got any more. And when they eat, they just find whatever they can find; they have to eat that; because, well, haven't got nothing else to eat. And these little fellows, when they--they have a cow, and they take a porcupine quill and stick it in its throat here, in its jugular vein and dra--drain what they call a--a gourd, but it isn't, it's made out of animal skins, a little sack, about like that. And they drain half full of blood, and then milk the rest of it with milk, and put a stick and churn it up and down like that, and it makes a cake like a--like a lollipop. That's what they have. And this little fellow had had his diet. And they wear no clothes, just a little bit of beads.
And so his little tummy here was just as bloody as it could be from where he'd eat his--his meal. And he--poor little fellow was cross-eyed, and he was standing there. And I said, "Now, 'course, anyone knows I couldn't heal the child." I said, "You can see it's cross-eyed." But I said, "God is the only One could heal it." I said, "I couldn't have nothing to do with it." I said, "As far as its little life, how--what happened--or what's--if there's anything in the way, God would perhaps show that to me." But I said, "Now, to heal it, I can't, 'cause I'm just a man."
text_no:
Deretter kom det en liten gutt gående. Noen av dere barna her, når dere går til fontenen for å drikke, tenker nok ikke over hvordan små afrikanske gutter må drikke. De legger seg rett ned og drikker fra hvilken som helst vannkilde de finner, selv om det er gjørmete. De har ikke noe annet valg. Når de spiser, spiser de hva enn de kan finne fordi de ikke har noe annet å spise.
Disse små guttene, hvis de har en ku, tar en pinnsvinpigg og stikker den inn i kuens jugularvene. Deretter drenerer de blod i en sekk laget av dyreskinn, omtrent på størrelse med en gourd. De fyller sekken halvfull med blod og melker resten med melk. Deretter rører de blandingen til det blir en slags kake, litt som en lollipop. Det er det de spiser. Denne lille gutten hadde spist sin måltid. De går uten klær, bortsett fra noen få perler. 
Den lille magen hans var blodig etter måltidet. Denne fattige lille gutten var skjeler, og han stod der. Jeg sa, "Nå, selvfølgelig, hvem som helst vet at jeg ikke kunne helbrede barnet." Jeg sa, "Dere kan se at han er skjeler. Men jeg sier, Gud er den eneste som kan helbrede ham." Jeg sa, "Jeg kan ikke gjøre noe med det. Når det gjelder hans liv, hvis det er noe i veien, kan Gud muligens vise det til meg." Men jeg sa, "Å helbrede ham kan jeg ikke, for jeg er bare et menneske."

text:
And I guess there was twenty doctors or more setting on the platform. The--the medical association give me the right hand of fellowship, right down through Africa. Said, "That's truly Divine healing." And so we had that all in papers, if anybody, any doctor happened to be present like to look it over, well, that's perfectly all right. See? We'd be glad to let you see it.
And so they--this little lad was standing there. And while I was looking at him, I said, "Why, I see…" I said, "The boy was born that way." I said, "He come that way."
And so--and the Zulu interpreter… They had fifteen different interpreters there for fifteen different tribes of people. So they--they… Zulu interpreter gave that, and the mother raised up that that was the truth, setting, the place out on the--the plain there. And so that was right; she raised up her hand. And then while I was looking at the little fellow, I looked right back at him again. He was out of a Christian family. I looked back, his little eyes was straight as mine or yours.
So I said, "Well, 'course anyone knows, he's already healed." He was standing about as far as that chair from me. I said, "He's healed. There's his eyes; you're looking at him." So I said, "Pass on little lad."
And the interpreter said, "Pass on."
text_no:
Og jeg antar det var tjue leger eller flere som satt på podiet. Medisinsk forening ga meg en velkomsthilsen helt gjennom Afrika. De sa: "Det er virkelig guddommelig helbredelse." Vi hadde det alt sammen i papirene, så hvis noen leger tilfeldigvis var til stede og ønsket å se det over, var det helt i orden. Vi ville med glede vise det.
Denne lille gutten sto der. Mens jeg så på ham, sa jeg: "Ja, jeg ser..." Jeg sa: "Gutten ble født slik. Han kom til verden slik."
Zulu-tolken var en av femten forskjellige tolker for femten ulike folkegrupper. Zulu-tolken gjenga det, og moren bekreftet at det var sant ved å rekke opp hånden sin mens hun satt på sletten. Det var korrekt. Mens jeg fortsatt så på den lille gutten, la jeg merke til at han kom fra en kristen familie. Da jeg så på ham igjen, var øynene hans like rette som mine eller dine.
Jeg sa: "Vel, selvfølgelig vet alle at han allerede er helbredet." Gutten sto omtrent like langt unna meg som den stolen der borte. Jeg sa: "Han er helbredet. Se selv, her er øynene hans." Så sa jeg: "Gå videre, lille gutt."
Og tolken sa: "Gå videre."

text:
And I heard an argument over here on my side, and I wondered what it was. And I was looking at someone else, a fixing to pray for them. And so then I happened to hear… There was a doctor trying to get over where I was at. And so Mr. Baxter told him, said, "No, you can't do that." And Mr. Bosworth was trying to get him away, and so they… He just kept on; he wanted to come anyhow.
And so I turned around, said, "What's the matter, Doctor?"
He said, "What'd you do to that boy, Reverend Branham? Did you hypnotize that child?"
I said, "Certainly not, certainly not. I couldn't hypnotize him. (See?) I…"
He said, "Well…"
I said, "If hypnotism will make his eyes straight…" I said, "You mean to tell me ya--ya--you licensed to practice medicine and--and don't know no more about hypnotism than that?" I said, "Well, if hypnotism would straighten a child's eye, you better start practicing hypnotism." See? I said, "It wasn't hypnotism; it was the power of God."
And they had beautiful, big lilies up in the part of the country where we were at. They had a bunch of them setting on the platform. I said… They… He said, "Now, I know God's in those lilies," said, "that's true." But said, "Now, God being out here visible to make that child's eyes straight, I can't see that."
I said, "Well now, the only thing you've got to do is… You can either take what I said, the boy's eyes…"
He said, "Well, I let him through the gate."
I said, "Then you know his eyes were crossed."
He said, "Sure, standing here looking at him."
And I said, "Well now, his are straight, isn't it?"
Said, "Yeah."
I said, "Jesus Christ," I said, "did it. So now, it's all in…?…"
So he said, "Just a moment." And they started to pull him back, Mr. Bosworth, and he said, "Just a moment," and he walked to the microphone, said, "I want to accept Jesus Christ as my personal Saviour too."
And then a little while later God gave a great miracle of a crippled man, and that's when the miracle of God was performed, when in that one day thirty thousand souls come to Christ.
text_no:
Jeg hørte en diskusjon på min side og lurte på hva det var. Jeg så på noen andre og gjorde meg klar til å be for dem. Da hørte jeg en lege prøve å komme bort der jeg var. Mr. Baxter fortalte ham: "Nei, du kan ikke gjøre det." Mr. Bosworth prøvde å få legen til å gå bort, men han insisterte på å komme fram.
Jeg snudde meg og spurte: "Hva er i veien, Doktor?"
Han svarte: "Hva gjorde du med den gutten, Pastor Branham? Hypnotiserte du ham?"
Jeg sa: "Absolutt ikke. Jeg kunne ikke hypnotisere ham."
Han repliserte: "Vel…"
Jeg sa: "Hvis hypnose kan rette opp øynene hans…" Jeg fortsatte: "Du mener å fortelle meg at du er lisensiert til å praktisere medisin og vet ikke mer om hypnose enn det? Hvis hypnose kunne rette opp et barns øyne, burde du begynne å praktisere hypnose. Det var ikke hypnose, det var Guds kraft."
Det var vakre, store liljer i området vi var i, og det var noen av dem på plattformen. Han sa: "Jeg vet at Gud er i de liljene, det er sant. Men at Gud skulle være synlig her for å rette opp det barnets øyne, det kan jeg ikke se."
Jeg sa: "Nå vel, det eneste du må gjøre er å akseptere det jeg sa. Gutten hadde kryssede øyne."
Han svarte: "Ja, jeg lot ham komme inn gjennom porten."
Jeg sa: "Da vet du at øynene hans var korsede."
Han svarte: "Ja, jeg så det."
Jeg sa: "Nå er øynene hans rette, ikke sant?"
Han sa: "Ja."
Jeg sa: "Jesus Kristus gjorde det. Nå, det er alt."
Han sa: "Et øyeblikk." De begynte å dra ham bort, men han fortsatte: "Et øyeblikk," og han gikk til mikrofonen og sa: "Jeg vil også akseptere Jesus Kristus som min personlige Frelser."
Litt senere ga Gud et stort mirakel med en mann som var krøpling, og da utførte Gud sitt mirakel, da tretti tusen sjeler den dagen kom til Kristus.

text:
Now, we have… This is Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday; four nights yet, and Sunday afternoon service. I certainly appreciate you people of coming out here on these cool nights, and they're… And a nice little audience for this city here, way out away from other cities and things. You have to drive, and this is two weeks. You don't know how I appreciate it, and how that you've been a great inspiration to me. I mean that from my heart.
Last night, God begin to speak to me about something after I left here. Now, I don't mean to say that now like that He came down to me in a vision; I'm very particular about that. And then this morning again, it came to me again. And, Brother Beeler, tomorrow night, if possible, I'll buy you a spool. I want what I say tomorrow night on a recording so I can send it to the "Voice of Healing," and every one of the religious papers. I want this article published, what I want to say tomorrow night; if you'll do that for me. It's something that I--I been warning; it's been coming to my heart for some time. I--I want to get it to you tomorrow night; it's concerning Divine healing; if God willing.
text_no:
Nå har vi... Dette er torsdag, fredag, lørdag og søndag; fire kvelder til, og søndags ettermiddagsmøte. Jeg setter virkelig pris på at dere kommer ut hit på disse kjølige kveldene. Vi har et fint lite publikum for denne byen, langt unna andre byer og ting. Dere må kjøre hit, og det har vært to uker. Dere aner ikke hvor mye jeg setter pris på det, og hvordan dere har vært en stor inspirasjon for meg. Jeg mener det fra hjertet.
I går kveld begynte Gud å tale til meg om noe etter at jeg dro herfra. Nå, jeg mener ikke å si at Han kom til meg i en visjon; jeg er veldig nøye med det. Og i morges kom det til meg igjen. Bror Beeler, i morgen kveld, om mulig, skal jeg kjøpe en spole til deg. Jeg vil ha det jeg sier i morgen kveld på et opptak, så jeg kan sende det til "Voice of Healing" og til alle de religiøse avisene. Jeg vil ha denne artikkelen publisert; det jeg vil si i morgen kveld. Kan du gjøre det for meg? Det er noe jeg har advart mot; det har vært i hjertet mitt en stund. Jeg vil dele det med dere i morgen kveld; det gjelder guddommelig helbredelse, om Gud vil.

text:
Tonight, I promised you I was going to talk about a--a Gospel subject and… See, Divine healing is--that's the bait that's on the hook. As I said the other night, you don't never show the fishy the--the hook, you show him the bait. And so we… That's what Diving healing… Jesus Christ, about eighty percent of His ministry was on Divine healing. But in there they watched and seen that He--what He done was--was of God. And then He told them the truth, and that's what won them to God. That's what is--the ministry's for. That's what God intends it to be for.
And now, tonight… Now, don't forget; tomorrow night I've got a--a special talk I want to say maybe just previous, or prior to my sermon, rather. And then, I promised every one of you here, that come that we was going to pray for everybody here if we possibly could. And God being my Helper, I'm going to stick to that, as I… See? I want to pray for every sick person who comes to be prayed for. I want to do my best to pray for every one of them, so I--before I leave the city. And I couldn't ask for no more time. The mayor of this city, and their board, and so forth has been very nice to us. We appreciate them, and the business men, and every one of the city, all the officials, to let us have this place, and we're--we're grateful to those men. And I pray that God will bless them in every way. Bless the little city in every way that it can be, and may you grow and prosper and God be with you in everything you do.
I just trust that you'll have an old fashion revival that every sinner in this city will become a Christian, is my prayer. Let him belong to whatever church he desires to, that's his business. I hope that every person comes to church and does what's right. That's…?… It's the… [Blank spot on tape]…?…
text_no:
I kveld lovet jeg å snakke om et emne innen evangeliet. Se, guddommelig helbredelse er agnet på kroken. Som jeg sa tidligere, du viser aldri fisken kroken, men agnet. Det er slik guddommelig helbredelse fungerer. Omtrent åtti prosent av Jesu kristi tjeneste handlet om guddommelig helbredelse. Men det var ved å se at det Han gjorde var av Gud, at folk ble vunnet for Gud. Det er hva tjenesten er til for, og det er det Gud vil at den skal være.
Og nå, i kveld… Ikke glem; i morgen kveld har jeg en spesiell tale som jeg vil holde rett før prekenen. Og jeg lovet hver eneste av dere som kommer, at vi skal be for alle som er her, om mulig. Med Guds hjelp, skal jeg holde fast ved det. Jeg vil be for hver syk person som kommer for å bli bedt for, og jeg vil gjøre mitt beste for å be for hver eneste av dem før jeg forlater byen. Jeg kunne ikke bedt om mer tid. Ordføreren i denne byen, sammen med styret og andre har vært veldig vennlige mot oss. Vi setter pris på dem, forretningsfolkene og hver enkelt i byen, alle tjenestemennene som har latt oss bruke dette stedet. Vi er takknemlige til disse mennene, og jeg ber om at Gud vil velsigne dem på alle måter. Jeg ber om at denne byen vil vokse og blomstre, og at Gud skal være med dere i alt dere gjør.
Jeg håper inderlig at dere vil oppleve en gammeldags vekkelse, og at hver eneste synder i denne byen vil bli en kristen. La ham tilhøre den menigheten han ønsker, det er hans egen sak. Jeg håper at alle kommer til menigheten og gjør det som er rett. Det er det som… [Tomt.område.på.lydbånd]

text:
… if you'd like to read it with me, or… why I'd be glad for you to do it. And now, said:
Let not your hearts be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.
In my Father's house is many mansions: if it were not so, I would have told you. I go and prepare a place for you.
And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.
And where I go you know, and the way you know.
Thomas saith unto him, Lord, we know not where thou goest; and how can we know the way?
Jesus said unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
If you'd known me, you should have known my Father also: and from henceforth you know him, and have seen him.
Philip saith unto him, Lord, shew us the Father, and it sufficeth us.
Now, may the Lord add His blessings as we pray. Our heavenly Father, we thank Thee tonight for the Blood of Jesus Christ, God's Son, that cleanses us from all sin. And knowing as we bow our heads to the dust from where we were taken, and knowing that someday we got to return back to that dust… The atoms that holds this body together will break some of these days through the corruption of the flesh. They'll be loose in this air where they was before they made up this body, but, God, You know where every one of them will be. That every man that's borned of the Spirit of God has Everlasting Life and shall never come into condemnation, but's pass from death unto Life. And we thank Thee for these things.
text_no:
... hvis du ønsker å lese sammen med meg, så ville jeg bli glad for det. Nå står det:
La ikke deres hjerter bli grepet av angst. Tro på Gud og tro på Meg.
I Min Fars hus er det mange rom. Var det ikke slik, ville Jeg ha sagt det til dere. Jeg går for å gjøre i stand et sted for dere.
Og når Jeg har gått og gjort i stand et sted for dere, vil Jeg komme tilbake og ta dere til Meg, for at også dere skal være der Jeg er.
Og hvor Jeg går, vet dere veien.
Thomas sa til Ham: Herre, vi vet ikke hvor Du går, hvordan kan vi da vite veien?
Jesus sa til ham: Jeg er veien, sannheten og livet. Ingen kommer til Faderen uten gjennom Meg.
Hadde dere kjent Meg, ville dere også kjent Min Far. Fra nå av kjenner dere Ham, og dere har sett Ham.
Filip sa til Ham: Herre, vis oss Faderen, så er det nok for oss.
Må Herren legge Sin velsignelse til dette mens vi ber. Vår himmelske Far, vi takker Deg i kveld for Jesu Kristi, Guds Sønns, Blod som renser oss fra all synd. Og når vi bøyer våre hoder mot støvet som vi ble tatt fra, vet vi at en dag må vi vende tilbake til det støvet… Atomene som holder denne kroppen sammen vil en dag brytes ned gjennom kjødets forråtnelse. De vil sveve i luften hvor de var før de dannet denne kroppen, men Gud, Du vet hvor hver eneste en av dem vil være. Hver mann som er født av Guds Ånd har Evig Liv og skal aldri komme til dom, men har gått over fra døden til Livet. Og vi takker Deg for disse tingene.

text:
Lord, our hearts tonight, as we look out across the world and see the great signs of the Scriptures being fulfilled, and see the worlds, as it was, staggering in with a headache band around it wondering what next. Great atomic weapons and hydrogen bombs and everything in the hands of sinful men, where one relay of that could blow the earth to--to bits… And knowing this, that the Bible has spoke so definitely of the last days, how these things would be…
Looking around at the nature of the people, seeing that we're in the spirit of the last day… They had to get in the spirit of the antediluvian world before they could be destroyed. They have to be in the spirit of war before they can have war. God, let us be in the Spirit of Christ tonight so we can have Christ. Let us be in the Spirit of repentance, a Spirit of faith. Give it to us tonight, our Father, of these great treasures of life. They're so freely, just by asking. And realizing that the greatest things of life, and the only real lasting things are the unseen…
And may Thy Spirit come tonight and fill every heart. Reclaim every backslider and save every sinner. And may the--those who are seeking the baptism of the Holy Spirit, may He come tonight and fill every heart. May the sick be healed, God receive glory. For we ask it in the Name of His beloved Son, Jesus our Lord. Amen.
text_no:
Herre, når vi i kveld ser ut over verden og ser de store tegnene fra Skriften oppfylles, og ser verden vakle som med et bånd rundt hodet, uvitende om hva som kommer neste. Store atomvåpen og hydrogenbomber i hendene på syndige mennesker, hvor én utløsermekanisme kan sprenge jorden i biter … Og vitende om at Bibelen har talt så tydelig om de siste dager, om hvordan disse tingene ville skje...
Ser vi rundt på menneskets natur, ser vi at vi lever i den siste tids ånd. De måtte komme inn i den antediluvianske verdens ånd før de kunne bli ødelagt. De må være i krigens ånd før de kan ha krig. Gud, la oss være i Kristi ånd i kveld, så vi kan få Kristus. La oss være i ånden av omvendelse, en ånd av tro. Gi oss disse store livsskattene i kveld, Far. De er så frie, bare ved å be om dem. 
Og å innse at de største tingene i livet, de eneste virkelig varige tingene, er de usette…
Må Din Ånd komme i kveld og fylle hvert hjerte. Gjenopprett hver frafallen og frels hver synder. Og de som søker dåpen i Den Hellige Ånd, må Han komme i kveld og fylle hvert hjerte. Må de syke bli helbredet, Gud få ære. For vi ber om dette i Hans elskede Sønns, vår Herre Jesu Navn. Amen.

text:
For our subject, I want to use this: "Show Us The Father And It'll Satisfy Us." The word "sufficeth" means "to satisfy."
Now, that's been the hungry call of the people for--for ages: to see God. How many of you here tonight would really like to see God? I'd just like to your hands. Would you like to see Him? Well, if by God's Word, and by God's help I can bring God right in this meeting that you can see Him, will you accept Him? That's what I want to ask you, if you… Well, there's a third of you would accept Him, anyhow. The rest of them wouldn't if you seen Him. [Brother Branham laughs] I just don't believe you raised your hands. I don't believe you meant that.
text_no:
La oss bruke dette som emne: "Vis Oss Faderen Og Det Vil Tilfredsstille Oss." Ordet "tilfredsstille" betyr "å gjøre fornøyd."
Dette har vært menneskets sultne rop gjennom tidene: å se Gud. Hvor mange av dere her i kveld vil virkelig ønske å se Gud? Kan jeg få se hendene deres? Vil dere se Ham? Vel, hvis jeg ved Guds Ord og med Guds hjelp kan bringe Gud inn i dette møtet slik at dere kan se Ham, vil dere da akseptere Ham? Det er det jeg vil spørre dere om. Vel, en tredjedel av dere ville akseptert Ham uansett. Resten av dere ville ikke akseptert Ham selv om dere så Ham. [Bror Branham ler] Jeg tror bare ikke dere rakk opp hendene. Jeg tror ikke dere mente det.

text:
But now, God is just as real as--as--as the light is--is here in this room. Now, we want to look at God tonight and see if we can see God.
Now, Thomas wanted to see Him here, or Philip, rather. He said, "Lord, show us the Father and it'll satisfy us. If You'll show me God, it'll satisfy me." Well now, that ought to be a satisfaction for anyone. If they could… "If God is so great, let's see Him. Let's see what He looks like, and that--that'll satisfy me if I can see Who He is and see what He looks like. I will know Who to worship and what He is." That sounds sensible and reasonable, doesn't it?
text_no: Men nå er Gud like virkelig som lyset i dette rommet. I kveld ønsker vi å se på Gud og se om vi kan se Ham. Thomas ønsket å se Ham, eller rettere sagt Filip. Han sa: "Herre, vis oss Faderen, så vil det tilfredsstille oss. Hvis Du viser meg Gud, vil det tilfredsstille meg." Vel, det burde være en tilfredsstillelse for alle. Hvis Gud er så stor, la oss se Ham. La oss se hvordan Han ser ut, og det vil tilfredsstille meg hvis jeg kan se Hvem Han er og hvordan Han ser ut. Da vil jeg vite hvem jeg skal tilbe og hva Han er." Det høres fornuftig og rimelig ut, gjør det ikke?

text: Now, let's just see if we can find God tonight in--in our midst now. Let's take of God in--in about three or four different ways and see if we can find Him. Let's speak of God first in His universe; let's speak of God in His Word; God in His Son; and then God in His people, and see if we can find Him here tonight. Now, I'm going to take those four subjects there and try to talk on them just for a few moments to see if we can see the Father.
text_no: La oss prøve å se om vi kan finne Gud i vår midte i kveld. La oss betrakte Gud på tre eller fire ulike måter og se om vi kan finne Ham. Først skal vi snakke om Gud i sitt univers; så Gud i sitt Ord; Gud i Sin Sønn; og deretter Gud i sitt folk, og se om vi kan finne Ham her i kveld. Jeg vil ta for meg disse fire temaene og snakke om dem i noen få øyeblikk for å se om vi kan se Faderen.

text:
Every man since the dawn of time has longed to see God, want to look at Him. Job one time said, "Oh, if I only knew where He was…" In other words, "If I knew where to go and knock on His door, and He'd open up the door, and I'd come in and set down and talk with Him…" That's the way people want to see God, and yet God was so close to Job till He was speaking to him.
And a little later He said, "Now, gird up your loins. I'm going to speak to you like a man." And God came down in a whirlwind and begin to speak to Job. And Job begin to see the flowers and nature.
text_no:
Helt siden tidens begynnelse har mennesket lengtet etter å se Gud, ønsket å se Ham. Job sa en gang: "Å, om jeg bare visste hvor Han var..." Med andre ord: "Hvis jeg visste hvor jeg skulle gå og banke på Hans dør, og Han åpnet døren, og jeg kunne komme inn, sette meg ned og snakke med Ham..." Folk ønsker å se Gud på denne måten, men Gud var så nær Job at Han talte til ham.
Litt senere sa Han: "Nå, spenn beltet om livet. Jeg skal tale til deg som en mann." Gud kom ned i en virvelvind og begynte å tale til Job. Job begynte å se blomstene og naturen.

text:
I want to speak on His universe first, because that was my first Bible. My people wasn't religious. And I… My first Bible was the universe, watching God. I don't believe there's any man that's got his right mind, or a woman, that could look in the face of a flower and say there is no such a thing as God. I believe it would show a mental deficiency on--on the--the part of the person, that could look at a big, beautiful calla lily and say there's no such a thing as God.
Why, all the science we've got in the world can't even make one little blade of grass. That's right. They can make something that looks like it, but they can't make the grass, because it's got life in it. See? Just how--how great God is, and how people look way over the top…
I think if you'll listen to this closely tonight now, and we will get our minds right down, Divine healing will be so simple for you after you see where God is and Who He is.
text_no:
Jeg vil først snakke om Hans univers, fordi det var min første Bibel. Min familie var ikke religiøs. Min første Bibel var universet, ved å betrakte Gud. Jeg tror ikke at det finnes noen mentalt friske menn eller kvinner som kan se på en blomsts ansikt og hevde at Gud ikke eksisterer. Jeg mener det viser en mental svakhet hos personer som kan se på en stor, vakker kalla-lilje og si at Gud ikke eksisterer.
Vitenskapen i verden kan ikke engang lage ett enkelt gresstrå. Det er sant. De kan lage noe som ligner, men de kan ikke skape gresset, fordi det har liv i seg. Forstår dere hvor stor Gud er, og hvordan folk overser det åpenbare?
Jeg tror at hvis du lytter nøye i kveld, og vi virkelig fokuserer, vil Guddommelig helbredelse bli så enkel for deg når du ser hvor Gud er og Hvem Han er.

text:
Now, Job, he watched the flowers and he seen how they would die and raise again, and then it would die and raise again. And he begin… In the 14th chapter of Job he said, "But if, when a man dies, he giveth up the ghost. Yea, he wasteth away and his sons come to mourn his death, and where is he?" He's gone. No one… He doesn't rise again. But he said, "Now, you take the little flower when it…"
Here, you women around here with your flower beds, they're beautiful. I admire them as I go through the streets and looked at them. At a motel where I've been staying out here, they have a beautiful flower garden. And now, if you'll notice, this fall some of them will be young, some will be old flowers. But when frost strikes it, it's death, that's all, whether they're young or old.
And that's the way death is to the human race. It strikes young and old just alike. No matter who you are, when death strikes, it's going to take you whether you're a infant or whether you're just in the adolescent age, the bloom of life, or whether you're old, or whatever it is. Death's no respect of persons. It comes to all.
text_no:
Job observerte blomstene og la merke til hvordan de døde og blomstret opp igjen. I Job kapittel 14 sier han: "Når en mann dør, gir han opp ånden. Ja, han visner bort, og hans sønner kommer for å sørge, men hvor er han?" Han er borte. Ingen ser ham stå opp igjen. Men Job sier videre: "Nå, se på den lille blomsten når den..."
Til dere kvinner med blomsterbed, de er vakre. Jeg beundrer dem når jeg går gjennom gatene og ser på dem. På motellet hvor jeg har bodd her, har de en vakker blomsterhage. Hvis dere legger merke til det til høsten, vil noen av blomstene være unge, andre gamle. Men når frosten kommer, er det døden for dem, uansett alder.
På samme måte er døden for menneskeheten. Den rammer både unge og gamle likt. Uansett hvem du er, vil døden komme til deg, enten du er et spedbarn, i ungdommen, i livets blomstring, eller gammel. Døden gjør ingen forskjell. Den kommer til oss alle.

text:
Well, when death strikes the little flower, you notice it bows its little head, just as reverent. The petals drop off, and the leaves drop off, and a little black seed falls out. Then they have a funeral procession. The rains come and cry down in the fall of the year, bury the little fellow.
And then the first thing you know the freeze comes. Maybe the little seed's laying about that deep under the ground where the fall's rains buried it. And then after while, the see--the real cold spell comes and it freezes. Then the little old seed freezes. The pulp burst, runs out of it. The seed bursts open and the pulp runs out. And then the winter continues on through the blizzards, and the blizzard after blizzard.
text_no:
Når døden inntreffer for den lille blomsten, legger du merke til at den bøyer sitt lille hode like ærbødig. Kronbladene faller av, bladene faller av, og et lite svart frø faller ut. Deretter har de en slags begravelsesprosesjon. Regnet kommer, gråter og dekker den lille planten om høsten.
Neste ting som skjer er at frosten kommer. Kanskje ligger det lille frøet omtrent så dypt under jorden som høstens regn har begravd det. Etter en stund kommer den virkelige kuldeperioden, og da fryser det lille frøet. Fruktkjøttet sprenges og renner ut. Og så fortsetter vinteren med snøstorm etter snøstorm.

text:
Then after while along about February, the last of February, first of March, the stalk's gone, the bulb's gone, the seed's gone, the pulp's gone, the petal's gone, the flower's gone. Everything there is is gone. Is that the end of that flower? No, sir. Just let that sun begin to warm that earth again and that flower will live again. Why? There's a germ of life in that flower that no man can find or no winter can freeze out. God has made it in His Own way, and that little germ of life is preserved in that ground, and that flower will live again.
Well, if God made a way for a flower to live again, what about a man who's made in His image? There's a way somewhere for that to live again.
text_no:
I slutten av februar, begynnelsen av mars, er stilken borte, knoppen borte, frøet borte, margen borte, kronbladet borte, blomsten borte. Alt er borte. Er det slutten på den blomsten? Nei, absolutt ikke. La solen begynne å varme opp jorden igjen, så vil blomsten leve igjen. Hvorfor? Det er en livsspire i blomsten som ingen kan finne eller vinteren kan fryse bort. Gud har skapt den på Sin Egen måte, og denne lille livsspiriten er bevart i jorden. Blomsten vil leve igjen.
Hvis Gud har skapt en måte for en blomst å leve igjen, hva da med et menneske som er skapt i Hans bilde? Det finnes en måte for at også mennesket skal kunne leve igjen.

text:
Here not long ago, I was sitting with old Dr. Spurgeon, an old Methodist minister. Now, he's a real good old brother. We were setting, eating some ice cream together. "The Agriculture Hour,' kind of a little 4-H club now has--has got a--a--a little machine that turns out corn. "The Agriculture Hour," they had it. And that little old… Many farmers are setting here probably know of it.
And that machine will run out a grain of corn so perfect that you can take a handful out of the sack that was run out of the machine and a handful out of the sack that was growed in the field and mix them together, you couldn't tell them apart if you had to. And this over here that the machine run will make just as good of corn meal as this one will. It'll make the same kind of corn flakes.
You take and cut it open and put in a laboratory, perhaps the skin on there, would look that thick under the microscope. The heart's in the right place. Put it under the analysis and it'll be the same amount of potash, the same amount of calcium, the same amount of moisture, just exactly the same. There's no way in the world of telling it, only one way: bury them. The one that man made just rots away and that's all of it. The one that God made has got a germ of life in it; it reproduces itself again.
text_no:
Nylig satt jeg sammen med Dr. Spurgeon, en aldrende metodistprest. Han er en veldig god bror. Vi spiste is sammen og snakket om "The Agriculture Hour," et lite 4-H klubbprogram som nå har en maskin som lager kunstig mais. Mange bønder her kjenner sikkert til den.
Denne maskinen kan produsere maiskorn som er så perfekte at hvis du tar en håndfull fra maskinen og en håndfull fra en sekk med ekte mais fra åkeren, kunne du ikke se forskjell. Maisen fra maskinen kan også brukes til å lage like god maismel eller frokostblandinger som den ekte maisen.
Hvis du kutter maiskornet og undersøker det i et laboratorium, ville skallet se like tykt ut under mikroskopet. Kjernen ville være på riktig plass. Analysene ville vise samme mengde kalium, kalsium og fuktighet. Det er ingen synlig forskjell, bortsett fra én avgjørende test: plante dem. Maisen laget av mennesker vil bare råtne bort. Maisen Gud har skapt inneholder et livskraftig korn som kan reprodusere seg selv.

text: Listen. A man can stand, both of them going to church and both of them professing Christianity, but except this man's borned again of the Spirit of God, it's finished. That's all there is to it. And as a germ of life, it comes into a man that he lives again, because God has germitized his spirit by Jesus Christ the resurrected Son of God. Amen. Certainly. Watch God in any way you want to.
text_no: Hør. En mann kan delta i menigheten, både han og en annen kan bekjenne seg som kristne, men hvis denne mannen ikke er gjenfødt av Guds Ånd, er det over. Det er alt som skal til. Når en livspirke kommer inn i en mann, lever han igjen, fordi Gud har spirkelagt hans ånd gjennom Jesus Kristus, den oppstandne Guds Sønn. Amen. Absolutt. Se på Gud på hvilken som helst måte du ønsker.

text:
Job begin to wonder why that men laid down and he didn't rise up. He didn't see him come up in the spring, but he seen the flower come up in the spring. Well then, when Elihu came to him and begin to speak to him, he said, "Now look, Job, you're accusing God wrong." He said, "Now, look. That flower--that flower has not sinned. It stands right in the way that God made it, and it reproduces itself right back from death to resurrection, death to resurrection." All right. He said, "Now, but man sinned, and that separated him from God and resurrection."
So he said, "But there's coming a just One." Amen. "There's coming One Who will stand in the breach for this sinner, that will put His hand on a sinful man and a Holy God, and bridge the way."
Oh, my. Job noticed it. He was a prophet, setting on the ash heap. He got up, shook himself. He'd been scraping his boils. And he got up and shook himself, said, "I know my Redeemer liveth." The lightnings begin to flash, the thunders begin to roar. The prophet got lined up with God. See? "I know my Redeemer liveth ('Liveth' is 'continuation.'), and at the last days He will stand upon the earth; and though the skin worms destroys this body, yet in my flesh shall I see God, Whom I shall see for myself. Mine eyes shall behold and not another." I believe he did that at the resurrection.
text_no:
Job begynte å undre seg over hvorfor mannen lå der uten å reise seg. Han så ikke mannen komme opp om våren, men han så blomsten spire om våren. Da Elihu kom til ham og begynte å snakke til ham, sa han: «Job, du anklager Gud feilaktig. Se, den blomsten har ikke syndet. Den står akkurat slik Gud skapte den, og den reproduserer seg selv fra død til oppstandelse, døds i oppstandelse.» 
Han fortsatte: «Men mennesket syndet, og det skilte ham fra Gud og oppstandelsen. Men det skal komme en rettferdig En. Det skal komme En som vil stå i bruddet for synderen, som vil legge Sin hånd på en syndig mann og på en Hellig Gud, og bygge bro.»
Job, som var en profet og satt på askehopen, skjønte det. Han reiste seg, ristet seg og hadde skrapt sine byller. Han ropte: «Jeg vet at min Gjenløser lever.» Lynene begynte å lyne, tordenen begynte å brøle, og profeten ble justert med Gud. «Jeg vet at min Gjenløser lever (’lever’ betyr ’fortsetter’), og på de siste dager skal Han stå på jorden; og selv om hudenormer ødelegger denne kroppen, skal jeg i mitt kjød se Gud, Ham skal jeg se for meg selv. Mine egne øyne skal se og ingen annen.» Jeg tror han opplevde dette ved oppstandelsen.

text:
And at the resurrection every person that's borned of the Spirit of God will come forth in the resurrection.
I believe that it'll be just as we are here. You'll be little men, big men, and--and red-headed, and black-headed, and whatever you are. Now, a lot of people think it's--everybody will be just, look just the same. God's a God of variety. He has white flowers, blue flowers, red flowers, pink flowers; big trees, little trees; big mountains, little mountains; plains. He's a God of variety.
And the--the principle of Christianity is based upon resurrection, not reincarnation, resurrection. If this belongs right here, this slip, and I drop that down. Now, that doesn't mean take this and put it in its place. That isn't a resurrection. No matter it looks just exactly like it, but that isn't it. Resurrection is to go down and get the same one and bring it up. The same Jesus that went into the grave, the same Jesus come out of the grave. That's right. It's a resurrection.
text_no:
Ved oppstandelsen vil hver person som er født av Guds Ånd komme frem i oppstandelsen. Jeg tror at det vil være som vi er her. Du vil være små menn, store menn, rødhårede eller svarthårede, akkurat som du er nå. Mange tror at alle vil se like ut, men Gud er en Gud av variasjon. Han har hvite, blå, røde og rosa blomster; store og små trær; høye og lave fjell; sletter. Han er en Gud av variasjon.
Kristendommens prinsipp er basert på oppstandelse, ikke reinkarnasjon. Oppstandelse handler om å hente det samme som gikk ned og bringe det opp igjen. Hvis denne lappen hører hjemme her, og jeg slipper den, betyr ikke det å ta noe som ser likt ut og sette det på samme plass. Det er ikke en oppstandelse. Oppstandelse er å gå ned og hente den samme lappen og bringe den opp. Den samme Jesus som gikk i graven, den samme Jesus kom ut av graven. Det er riktig. Det er en oppstandelse.

text:
God made angels. Mama used to tell me I'd be an angel when I died. I found out God never made me an angel. He made me a man, and I will always be a man. I will never be an angel. God made Angels, and He made Seraphims, and He made--He made trees, and He made man, and we're… It's just resurrection. That's what it is, what Jesus Christ proved.
Now, to the Mohammedan and their superstitions, and whatever it is, yet God in His universe proves to us that the Christian religion is right, because it's based on resurrection. Watch everything else how it resurrects, His whole creation.
Now, look at God and see if we can look at Him.
text_no:
Gud skapte engler. Mamma pleide å si at jeg ville bli en engel når jeg døde, men jeg har funnet ut at Gud aldri gjorde meg til en engel. Han skapte meg som et menneske, og jeg vil alltid være et menneske. Jeg vil aldri bli en engel. Gud skapte engler, serafim, trær og mennesker. Det handler om oppstandelse; det er det Jesus Kristus beviste.
Selv for de muslimske tradisjonene og deres overtro, viser Gud gjennom sitt univers at den kristne troen er sann fordi den er basert på oppstandelsen. Se på alt annet hvordan det oppstår igjen, hele Hans skapning.
La oss se på Gud og undersøke om vi kan få et glimt av Ham.

text:
Here not long ago, a little boy was going to Sunday school down in our city and to a very prominent church. And he--he heard so much about God till he said to his mother; he said, "Mother, I want to ask you something." She said… "Could anybody see God?" He said, "He's so great, I--I'd like to know if somebody could see Him."
"Why," she said, "sonny, ask your Sunday school teacher." So they asked the Sunday school and he…
She said, "Well, I don't know." Said, "You better ask the pastor."
So the--the--the pastor was coming home with them for lunch and he said, "Pastor, I want to ask you a question." Said, "Can anyone see God?"
"Oh," he said, "no, of course not. Why," he said, "no man can see God." Said, "No one can see Him."
text_no:
For en tid siden skulle en liten gutt gå på søndagsskole i vår by, til en meget prominent menighet. Han hadde hørt så mye om Gud at han sa til sin mor: "Mor, jeg vil spørre deg om noe." Hun svarte: "Ja, hva da?" Gutten spurte: "Kan noen se Gud? Han er så stor; jeg vil gjerne vite om noen kan se Ham."
Hun sa: "Vennen min, spør søndagsskolelæreren din." Så spurte de søndagsskolelæreren, men hun sa: "Jeg vet ikke, spør heller pastoren."
Pastoren skulle spise lunsj med dem, så gutten spurte: "Pastor, jeg vil stille Dem et spørsmål. Kan noen se Gud?"
Pastoren svarte: "Å nei, selvfølgelig ikke. Ingen kan se Gud. Nei, ingen kan se Ham."

text:
Well, the little fellow was disappointed. And he used to stay down on the river. We live near the Ohio river, and he used to go with an old fisherman up the river, a piece towards the Six Mile Island, fishing all the time. And one day after a big rain, along this time of year, he was coming, pulling down through the waters, you know. And any boatman knows how the music is to… after the rain is over and the little bitty waves on the water, and let your oars clip across it as you're pulling your boat. It was clipping along like that.
The old fisherman had a long, white beard. He begin to look over his shoulder. And where the sun come out, where the rain had been, made a rainbow. And he'd pull the oars, you know, and he'd turn around and look. The little fellow noticed tears dripping off his white beard, as it dropped down. He'd pull a little farther; he'd look back. And the…
text_no:
Den lille gutten var skuffet. Han pleide å tilbringe tid ved elven. Vi bor nær Ohio-elven, og han pleide å gå med en eldre fisker oppover elven, mot Six Mile Island, for å fiske. En dag etter et stort regnskyll, på denne tiden av året, kom han roende nedover elven. Enhver båtfører vet hvordan lyden er etter at regnet har stoppet, med små bølger på vannet og årene som sklir over dem mens du ror båten. Årene lagde den karakteristiske lyden.
Den gamle fiskeren hadde et langt, hvitt skjegg. Han begynte å se over skulderen sin. Solen kom frem gjennom regnet og dannet en regnbue. Han trakk årene og snudde seg rundt for å se. Den lille gutten la merke til tårer som dryppet fra det hvite skjegget hans og ned i vannet. Han rodde litt videre, snudde seg igjen og så tilbake.

text:
Oh, the little boy got… He was setting in the bow of the boat, so he run up in the--towards the stern of the boat. He said, "Sir, I'm going to ask you a question that my mother can't answer, my Sunday school teacher, or my pastor." He said, "God is so great." Said, "Can anyone see Him?"
And the old fisherman pulled his oars into his lap, throwed his hands to his face like that and wept out, grabbed the little fellow and hugged him. He said, "God bless your little heart, honey. All I've seen for the past thirty-five years has been God." There's so much God on the inside, you can see Him on the outside if He gets on the inside. The old fellow was just so full of God in here, everything he looked at he seen God in it. You got to get God in here in order to see God outside.
text_no:
Den lille gutten satt i baugen av båten, men løp mot akterenden. Han sa, "Unnskyld, jeg har et spørsmål som verken mor, søndagsskolelæreren eller pastoren min kan svare på." Han fortsatte, "Gud er så stor. Er det mulig for noen å se Ham?"
Den gamle fiskeren la årene i fanget, tok hendene til ansiktet og begynte å gråte. Han grep den lille gutten og klemte ham. "Gud velsigne ditt lille hjerte, kjære. Alt jeg har sett de siste trettifem årene har vært Gud." Når det er så mye av Gud på innsiden, kan du se Ham på utsiden også. Den gamle mannen var så fylt av Gud inni seg at alt han så på, avslørte Gud for ham. Du må ha Gud inni deg for å kunne se Ham utenfor.

text:
You remember when you was first converted? Oh, how different things looked. The trees looked different and the birds had a sound like they never had before. Is that right? God had just come in here; God's speaking out in His universe. How marvelous He is.
How I--I love to watch Him in His nature, God how He--He provides things. Of the fall of the year, sometimes I go up into the north woods to go hunting. And I noticed up there on the lakes, these little ducks come up from down in the south and go up there and make their nests on this lake. And they… The little ducks are raised there; they're hatched out.
Well about, along about September it gets cold, and the first breeze comes down across the mountain there, and real cold breeze… Well them little ducks, now, has never left this lake. It was born right there on this lake. And there's one of those little ducks in that group is a leader, a little drake. And when that wind begins to blow down across through there, that little fellow will run out there in the middle of that lake, stick that little honker of his up in the air, honk four or five times and every duck on the pond will come to him.
text_no:
Husker du når du først ble omvendt? Å, hvor annerledes alt virket. Trærne så annerledes ut, og fuglesangen hørtes helt ny ut. Er det ikke sant? Gud hadde akkurat kommet inn i hjertet; Gud talte ut i Sin skaperverk. Hvor underfull Han er.
Jeg elsker å se Ham i naturen og hvordan Han sørger for alt. Om høsten drar jeg noen ganger til skogene i nord for å jakte. Der oppe ved innsjøene legger jeg merke til de små endene som kommer fra sør for å bygge rede. Disse endene klekkes og vokser opp der.
Rundt september blir det kaldt, og den første kalde vinden kommer ned over fjellet. Disse små endene har aldri forlatt innsjøen hvor de ble født. En av dem er en leder, en liten andrik. Når vinden begynner å blåse, løper denne lille fyren ut i midten av innsjøen, stikker nebbet opp i luften og roper. Da kommer alle endene til ham.

text:
And what he will do, he's never been off that lake; he was borned there in the spring; it's fall of the year; he will raise right up off of that lake and go just as straight to Texas as he can go, to the rice field, without a compass or anything. What is it? God working in His nature, working among His animals.
If God give a duck enough sense to know how to get out of the cold weather, what ought it do to a man that's borned again of the Spirit of God? See? Watch God among His ducks, among His--His--His animals.
text_no:
Han har aldri forlatt den innsjøen; han ble født der om våren. Nå er det høst, og han vil reise seg fra innsjøen og fly rett til risåkrene i Texas, uten kompass eller noen andre hjelpemidler. Hva er dette? Gud som arbeider gjennom Sin natur, som virker blant Sine skapninger.
Hvis Gud har gitt en and nok forstand til å vite hvordan den skal unnslippe kulden, hva bør da en gjenfødt mann, født på nytt ved Guds Ånd, gjøre? Se på Gud blant sine ender, blant Hans skapninger.

text:
You take for instance, like, you'll read in the newspaper and it says, "Tomorrow it's going to be a--it's going to be a pretty day." And watch that old hog go sow, go over there on the other side the hill, on the north side, bring them shucks around on the south side and make her bed; don't you pay any attention what that newspaper's talking about. That hog knows more about it than what the newspaper ever could know about it. It's going to get cold weather.
You go hunting and watch those rabbits getting back under those shelters and things like that. Look out for rainy weather. See? God working in His nature… Don't you believe He does? Look at Him in His sunset. Notice the sunset.
text_no:
For eksempel kan du lese i avisen at "I morgen blir det en fin dag." Men hvis du ser at grisen går til den andre siden av bakken, til nordsiden, og drar hamsene til sørsiden for å lage seg et rede, bør du ikke bry deg om hva avisen sier. Grisen vet mer om været enn avisen noen gang kan vite. Det kommer til å bli kaldt.
Når du går på jakt og ser at harene trekker seg tilbake under ly, kan du forvente regnvær. Ser du? Gud arbeider i Sin natur. Tror du ikke det? Se på Ham i solnedgangen. Legg merke til solnedgangen.

text:
Here some time ago, I was up in Colorado. It was early in the fall and we were hunting. Oh, how I love to get into those mountains. My, after all of those hard meetings, I go way back and get on a horse, about thirty-five miles from civilization, way back across the rocky peaks by myself, just take couple pack horses and take off. Get back there, not so much to hunt the animals, but to be alone with God; right out among His nature, that's where I see Him.
And I remember that year, specifically, there'd been kind of a warm season. The snow hadn't come high yet, to run the elk down. I was way high, way up around almost in the shale rock. I was walking along through the evergreens that day. And it--it'll storm awhile, and then it'll snow awhile, and then it'll… the sun will come out in the fall weather, up high in the mountains.
text_no:
For en tid tilbake var jeg i Colorado tidlig på høsten for å jakte. Å, hvor jeg elsker å være i fjellene. Etter alle de harde møtene, drar jeg langt tilbake, omtrent femtifem kilometer fra sivilisasjonen, alene med bare et par pakkhester. Jeg reiser ikke så mye for å jakte på dyrene, men for å være alene med Gud; ute i naturen, der jeg virkelig ser Ham.
Jeg husker spesielt det året fordi det hadde vært en varm sesong. Snøen hadde enda ikke kommet høyt nok til å drive elgene ned. Jeg var høyt oppe, nesten ved skiferklippene, og gikk blant grantrærne. Været skiftet mellom storm, snøfall og solskinn, som det ofte gjør om høsten i fjellene.

text:
And come up a storm, a real severe storm. And I got in behind a tree, and waiting there just a little bit till the storm blew over. And the first thing you know, the twister went through.
And after the storm let up, I got out from behind the tree and I stood there. Way back in the west the sun was beginning to go across the crevices of the rock, peeping through, that great all-seeing eye of God moving down like that. And I noticed where the evergreens was froze, where the rain had been, it formed a rainbow across the valley.
Brother, you talk about deep calling to the deep. I looked at that; I set my rifle against a tree; I raised up my hands. I said, "O great Jehovah, how wonderful." I seen the rainbow. "Could you see God in the rainbow?" Certainly. God gave that as a covenant; He'd no more destroy the world with water. God was in the rainbow.
Then I see it in Revelations 1, when the rainbow was across the throne, where Jesus was standing: a covenant God had made with the people. He was to look upon as jasper and sardius stone; the First, the Last; He that was, which is, and shall come; the Root and Offspring of David; the Morning Star. And the rainbow, seven church ages, the covenant made with His own Blood that He would save the people that would call on His Name.
text_no:
En kraftig storm brøt ut, en virkelig alvorlig storm. Jeg søkte ly bak et tre og ventet til stormen hadde blåst over. Før jeg visste ordet av det, hadde tornadoen passert.
Da stormen hadde roet seg, kom jeg frem fra skjulestedet og stod der. Langt tilbake i vest begynte solen å dukke opp bak sprekkene i fjellet, og dens store, allseende øye, som er Guds, beveget seg nedover. Jeg la merke til at der hvor eviggrønne trær var frosset etter regnet, hadde det dannet seg en regnbue over dalen.
Bror, du snakker om dypt som kaller på dypt. Jeg så på dette synet; jeg satte riflen mot et tre og løftet hendene opp. Jeg sa, "Å, store Jehova, hvor vidunderlig." Jeg så regnbuen. "Kan du se Gud i regnbuen?" Selvfølgelig. Gud ga den som et paktstegn; Han ville ikke lenger ødelegge verden med vann. Gud var i regnbuen.
Så ser jeg det i Åpenbaringen 1, hvor regnbuen var over tronen, der Jesus stod: en pakt Gud hadde inngått med folket. Han skulle sees som jaspis og sarderstein; Begynnelsen og Enden; Han som var, som er, og som skal komme; Davids rot og ætling; Morgenstjernen. Og regnbuen representerte de syv menighetsalderne, pakten beseglet med Hans eget Blod, slik at Han ville frelse alle som påkalte Hans Navn.

text:
Oh, how marvelous. I looked at that. Tears began to run down my cheeks. I listened, and way over here… The elk herd had all got scattered during the time of the storm. The old males would bugle, you know, and you could hear them bugling one to another.
My mother's a half-breed, a half Cherokee Indian. And there's just enough about me to make me love that nature, something down in there calling out. My conversion didn't even get to it.
Look. Then an old gray wolf on the mountain begin to howl and it's mate answered in the bottom. You talk about crying. Something took place. I couldn't hold myself still. It was God to me, screaming through His nature. Yes, I believe God is in His universe.
text_no:
Å, hvor fantastisk. Jeg så på det. Tårene begynte å renne nedover kinnene mine. Jeg lyttet, og langt borte her... Elgflokken hadde blitt spredt under stormen. De gamle hannene ropte, og du kunne høre dem rope til hverandre.
Min mor er halvt cherokee-indianer. Det er akkurat nok i meg til å få meg til å elske naturen, noe dypt der inne som roper ut. Min omvendelse nådde ikke engang til det punktet.
Se. Så begynte en gammel grå ulv på fjellet å ule, og følget dens svarte nede i dalen. Du kan bare forestille deg gråten. Noe skjedde. Jeg klarte ikke å holde meg rolig. Det var Gud for meg, som skrek gjennom Sin natur. Ja, jeg tror Gud er i Sitt univers.

text:
I stood there, and I run around, around, around the tree screaming to top of my voice. Somebody come by there, thought they had a maniac out in the woods. I didn't care what they thought. I was having a good time. I was thirty-five miles from a living soul, as far as I know, and I was praising God. I didn't care was anybody. I was praising Him. I wanted Him to know I love Him.
Around, around, around the tree I went. I stopped, and I'd shake my hands and scream and cry; and around, around, around the tree I'd go again. I was having a good time. Amen. I could see my Lord in His universe. I seen His rainbow; I seen His great sun, yonder. I looked at Him. I heard Him scream over yonder in the wolf. I heard Him answer down here. I heard the elk. I heard Him in there. God's everywhere. If you just look around, He's all over everywhere.
text_no:
Jeg stod der og løp rundt, rundt treet, mens jeg skrek av full hals. Noen kom forbi og trodde de hadde funnet en galning ute i skogen. Jeg brydde meg ikke om hva de tenkte. Jeg hadde en fantastisk tid. Jeg var trettifem kilometer unna noen levende sjel, så vidt jeg visste, og jeg priste Gud. Jeg brydde meg ikke om noe annet. Jeg priste Ham. Jeg ønsket at Han skulle vite at jeg elsket Ham.
Rundt, rundt, rundt treet gikk jeg. Jeg stoppet, ristet med hendene, skrek og gråt, og fortsatte å løpe rundt treet igjen. Jeg hadde en fantastisk tid. Amen. Jeg kunne se min Herre i Hans univers. Jeg så Hans regnbue, jeg så Hans store sol der ute. Jeg så på Ham. Jeg hørte Ham rope i ulvens hyl der borte. Jeg hørte Ham svare her nede. Jeg hørte elgen. Jeg hørte Ham der. Gud er overalt. Hvis du bare ser deg rundt, er Han overalt.

text:
Now, I happened to hear a noise. And I looked over there and there was a little old pine squirrel. I don't guess you people, unless you been in the mountains, know what one is. They call them little mountain jacks. They're little bitty fellows about like that, and the noisiest thing there is in the woods, holler at everything.
He was standing on an old stump. There was a blow down there. He was setting upon an old stump going chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter; chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter. I thought, "What's you so excited about, little fellow?" I thought he… it was 'cause I was screaming and carrying on. So I thought, "I'm not going to hurt you."
text_no:
Jeg hørte en lyd og så over dit. Der sto en liten gammel furuekorn. Dere kjenner det nok ikke med mindre dere har vært i fjellene. De kalles små fjellgeiter. De er bittesmå, omtrent slik, og de lager mest støy i skogen, roper på alt mulig.
Den sto på en gammel stubbe, der det var et vindfall. Den satt på stubben og kjeftet i vei: chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter; chatter, chatter, chatter, chatter. Jeg tenkte, "Hva er du så opphisset over, lille venn?" Jeg trodde det var fordi jeg ropte og bar meg. Så tenkte jeg, "Jeg skal ikke skade deg."

text:
But I happened to notice coming out from under the--the--the blow down, where the trees had blowed over years ago, a blow down, there was some stuff there, and there'd been a great, big American eagle had been forced down in the storm, and it was under that drift. That's what the little fellow was excited about.
So the big eagle jumped out of there, and he set up on there and he looked at me. I admired him. And I thought, "God, why did You stop me over here to show me something here?" I believe God's everywhere, if you'll just look around Him. I believe He's here right now. He's everywhere, if you'll just look around. And God is in His universe.
How could that world tip and turn and everything, just exactly the way it does, and revolve to the same time through the solar system? My, you couldn't--you couldn't believe that without believing in God. How great He is.
text_no:
Jeg la merke til noe som kom frem fra under trefallene, der trærne hadde blåst over for mange år siden. En svær amerikansk ørn hadde blitt tvunget ned av stormen og lå under vrakgodset. Det var dette den lille karen var så opphisset over.
Den store ørnen hoppet ut derfra, satte seg opp og så på meg. Jeg beundret den. Jeg tenkte: "Gud, hvorfor stoppet Du meg her for å vise meg dette?" Jeg tror Gud er overalt, hvis du bare ser etter. Jeg tror Han er her akkurat nå. Han er overalt, bare man ser seg rundt. Gud er til stede i Sitt univers.
Hvordan kan verden tippe og snu og alt, akkurat som det gjør, og rotere nøyaktig samtidig gjennom solsystemet? Man kan ikke tro det uten å tro på Gud. Hvor stor Han er.

text:
I wondered why God attracted my attention to that old eagle. I looked at him then. Why, I thought, "He's just an old scavenger. Why do… Why would I be attracted to him?" But I noticed him, one thing, his bravery. My, and I thought, "Well, that's what the little--the little squirrel's a barking at; it's that eagle."
Well, I looked at him. He'd look over at that squirrel, then he'd look over to me; he'd look over at the squirrel, and he look over me. I thought, "Well, are you scared?" And he… Them great big eyes flashed and looked at me, look back to the squirrel. And I thought, "Well, God, one thing, I believe You're in that wolf call; I believe You're in the rainbow; I believe You're in these evergreens; I believe You're in this wind that's whistling through these trees. Now, where You at in that eagle?"
But I noticed him: he was brave. There's where He was at. God don't like cowards. He wants men that's got backbone instead of a wishbone, somebody who says, "God's Word is right, and God's true, and let everything else be a lie."
As Eddie Perronet said…
All hail the power of Jesus' Name!
Let angels prostrate fall;
Bring forth the royal diadem,
And crown Him Lord of all.
All around my soul gives way,
Then He's all my hope and stay.
On Christ, the solid Rock, I stand;
All other ground is sinking sand.
That's right. God wants men and women who's got courage, and courageous enough to stand out and call black black and white white. That's right. Take that wishbone out and put a backbone in there, something that God can move on. That's right.
text_no:
Jeg undret meg over hvorfor Gud fikk min oppmerksomhet rettet mot den gamle ørnen. Jeg så på den og tenkte: "Han er jo bare en gammel åtselsfugl. Hvorfor... hvorfor skulle den tiltrekke meg?" Men jeg la merke til én ting: hans mot. Jeg tenkte, "Det er det den lille ekornet bjeffer på; det er ørnen."
Jeg så nøye på ørnen. Den så både på ekornet og på meg. Jeg spurte meg selv: "Er du redd?" Men de store øynene lynte mot meg før de vendte tilbake til ekornet. Jeg tenkte, "Gud, jeg tror Du er i ulvehylet; jeg tror Du er i regnbuen; jeg tror Du er i disse eviggrønne trærne og vinden som suser gjennom dem. Nå, hvor er Du i denne ørnen?"
Men jeg la merke til at ørnen var modig. Der var Han. Gud liker ikke feiginger. Han ønsker seg menn med ryggrad, ikke ønskeben, de som sier: "Guds Ord er sant, og alt annet er løgn."
Som Eddie Perronet sa...
All hail the power of Jesus' Name!
Let angels prostrate fall;
Bring forth the royal diadem,
And crown Him Lord of all.
All around my soul gives way,
Then He's all my hope and stay.
On Christ, the solid Rock, I stand;
All other ground is sinking sand.
Gud ønsker menn og kvinner med mot, som er dristige nok til å kalle svart for svart og hvit for hvit. Ta ut ønskebenet og sett inn en ryggrad, noe Gud kan bevege Seg på. Det er riktig.

text:
Then I noticed that fellow how he was standing there, how courageous, just as brave as he could be. I looked at him. I thought, "Well now, he ain't scared of that. He's scared of me." So I thought, "Boy, I won't hurt you. I admire you. Anything with that courage, I admire them."
I thought, "I will see if he's scared of me." And I--I said, "Say, you know I could shoot you?"
Looked over at me as if to say, "You think so?"
So I grabbed my rifle like that. He batted them big eyes. He looked at me again. He just kept his eyes right on me. I wondered, "Why don't he--why don't he get away? Why don't he fly?" Well, I kept… "Well, what makes you so brave?" And I happened to notice. I caught on. God showed me. Just keep looking at nature; you'll find Him. Just keep looking around there at something. You'll see Him.
I noticed him. He kept going like this with his wings. He was restless, them wings. I thought, "I see it, Lord. God, You gave him two wings." God gave that eagle wings, and he had confidence in the gift of God that had been given to him.
text_no:
Da la jeg merke til den karen, hvordan han sto der, så modig som han kunne være. Jeg så på ham og tenkte: "Han er ikke redd for det. Han er redd for meg." Så tenkte jeg: "Gutt, jeg skal ikke skade deg. Jeg beundrer deg. Alt med sånn mot, det beundrer jeg."
Jeg tenkte: "Jeg skal se om han er redd for meg." Og jeg sa: "Sier du det, vet du at jeg kan skyte deg?" 
Han så på meg som for å si: "Tror du det?" 
Så grep jeg riflen min sånn. Han sperret opp de store øynene. Han så på meg igjen og holdt blikket på meg. Jeg undret meg: "Hvorfor flyr han ikke av gårde? Hvorfor flyr han ikke?" Jeg fortsatte: "Hva gjør deg så modig?" Og da merket jeg det. Gud viste meg det. Bare fortsett å se på naturen; du vil finne Ham. Bare fortsett å se rundt deg på noe. Du vil se Ham.
Jeg merket at han fortsatte å bevege vingene sine, rastløs. Jeg tenkte: "Jeg forstår det, Herre. Gud, Du ga ham to vinger." Gud ga den ørnen vinger, og han hadde tillit til Guds gave som var blitt gitt ham.

text:
After while he got tired of looking at me there, so he just made one great big jump like that. And he made about two flops, and he was out into that timber. He never moved his wings another time. He just set his wings into the wind like that. And he just begin, every time a wave would come, it'd lift him up. And every time another wave would come, it'd lift him up. He wasn't patter-patter here and patter-patter there. He just let the air waves carry him on till he went plumb out of sight. I stood there and cried like a baby. I said, "O God, let me know how to set my wings in Your faith. Let the Holy Ghost waves carry me on, on, on, on and on. Hallelujah. Yes, sir.
He left that little old earth bound chatter, chatter here and chatter, chatter there. If we just knowed how to set our faith in God's power and move on out of here and let these people chatter, chatter, saying, "Days of miracles is past; there's no such a thing as Divine healing." Fly away from it. Hallelujah. Ride on every wave that comes. Just move up and raise up higher, higher. Just move out.
text_no:
Etter en stund ble han lei av å se på meg, så han tok et stort sprang. Han fløy et par omganger og forsvant inn i skogen uten å bevege vingene mer. Han satte vingene sine mot vinden og lot bølgene løfte seg opp hver gang de kom. Han flakset ikke her og der; han lot luftbølgene bære ham til han forsvant ut av syne. Jeg stod der og gråt som et barn og sa: "Å Gud, la meg lære å sette vingene mine i Din tro. La Den Hellige Ånds bølger bære meg videre, videre og videre. Halleluja. Ja, sir."
Han forlot den lille, jordbundne skravlingen her og der. Om vi bare visste hvordan vi setter vår tro på Guds kraft og beveger oss bort fra dette og lar folk skravle, skravle og si: "Miraklenes tid er forbi; det finnes ikke noe slikt som guddommelig helbredelse." Flykt fra det. Halleluja. Ri på hver bølge som kommer. Bare beveg deg oppover, oppover. Fortsett å stige høyere.

text:
God is in His universe. He certainly is. I watched him till he got plumb out of sight, and I thought, "O, God, I… It's just good to be here. Let's build three tabernacles." Watching God in His universe, watch Him among His nature, seeing what He's doing. God's moving in His treetops.
David said one time, he was laying there, he was waiting. And he said he heard the wind blowing between the mulberry limbs, and he was ready to go on. I tell you: if there ever was a time we need a moving in nature and moving in men, is today. That's right.
text_no:
Gud er i Hans univers. Det er Han absolutt. Jeg fulgte Ham til Han forsvant helt fra synet, og jeg tenkte: "Å, Gud, det er bare godt å være her. La oss bygge tre tabernakler." Å se på Gud i Hans univers, observere Ham i naturen, se hva Han gjør. Gud beveger seg i tretoppene.
